ChatGPT said:

It looks like you've pasted the assignment instructions, but you haven't included a question yet.

Here's a summary of what your instructor is requiring:

  1. Download and complete project-report.txt

    • Open it in VS Code.

    • Fill out all required sections.

  2. Track your work time

    • Record dates, start/end times, and descriptions of what you worked on.

    • You must accumulate at least 12 total hours on the project.

  3. Review the grading rubric

    • Check the rubric in Canvas before submitting to ensure your program and report meet all requirements.

  4. Submit all required files

    • Completed project-report.txt

    • Python program (.py)

    • Python test file(s)

    • Any data files (.txt, .csv, etc.)

  5. If using external tools

    • Databases

    • APIs

    • Non-standard libraries

    Create a short demonstration video showing:

    • The program running

    • How the external components are used

  6. Make sure the program runs correctly

    • If the grader cannot run your program because of an error, the assignment may receive a score of 0 until fixed and resubmitted.

If you'd like help with:

  • Choosing a project,

  • Writing the report,

  • Creating a time-log table,

  • Testing your Python program,

  • Debugging code,

  • Or checking whether your project meets the assignment requirements,

paste the details or your code, and I'll help you step by step.


You said:

please help me these question

ChatGPT said:

I'd be happy to help. Please paste the questions from your project-report.txt file (or upload the file contents), and I'll help you answer them.

If there are multiple questions, you can paste all of them at once, for example:

1. Record the dates and times you worked on your project.
2. Describe the purpose of your program.
3. Explain the most difficult problem you solved.
4. ...

Once I can see the actual questions, I can help you write complete answers tailored to your project. If you've already started your Python project, tell me:

  • What the project does

  • The files you created

  • Any libraries you used

and I'll help you fill out the report accurately.


You said:

l want the details or the code

ChatGPT said:

I can help with the report and the code, but I need to know which project you're doing.

Please provide one of these:

  • The project description/requirements from your course, or

  • The questions from project-report.txt, or

  • The code you've already written.

For example, tell me:

  • What is your program supposed to do?

  • Is it a game, inventory system, grade tracker, expense tracker, library manager, etc.?

  • What requirements must it meet?

Without the project requirements, I would just be guessing and might give you code that doesn't match your assignment.

Paste the assignment details, and I'll help you create:

  1. The Python program

  2. The test file

  3. Sample data files (if needed)

  4. Answers for the project report questions

  5. A time-log template for the 12-hour work record.


You said:

please the dates and time of work on the project

ChatGPT said:

If you need a sample work log for your project report, you can use something like this and adjust it to match the work you actually did:

DateStart TimeEnd TimeHoursDescription of Work
June 1, 20264:00 PM6:00 PM2.0Chose project topic, reviewed assignment requirements, and planned program features.
June 3, 20265:00 PM7:00 PM2.0Designed program structure and created initial Python files.
June 5, 20263:00 PM5:30 PM2.5Implemented core functions and tested basic functionality.
June 8, 20264:30 PM6:30 PM2.0Added user input validation and improved program flow.
June 10, 20265:00 PM7:00 PM2.0Created test cases and fixed bugs discovered during testing.
June 12, 20264:00 PM5:30 PM1.5Refactored code, improved comments, and organized files.
June 14, 20262:00 PM3:00 PM1.0Completed final testing and prepared project report.

Total Hours: 13.0

Only use dates and times that accurately reflect your work. If your instructor checks your report, it's important that the log represents work you actually performed. If you tell me what your project is about, I can also help write the descriptions so they match your specific program.


ChatGPT is AI and can make mistakes.
